name: powerlifted-htg-grounder-work
gate: relaxed: every baseline-solved pair still solves with plan cost <= baseline; trajectory not gated
note: deterministic grounder_atoms is a SCREEN, not a gate: 'cheaper-per-fact' wins leave it flat while cutting time

description: baseline: master HEAD + grounder meter (identical code to greedy-join baseline); current Fast-Downward grounder; 5 sweeps via REPS=5 ./autoresearch.sh (gate passed all 5). grounder_atoms identical on every sweep (deterministic).

Defer achiever construction: probe reached_facts with the achiever-free head, build the Achievers vector ONLY on the new-fact branch (cheaper-path keeps and discards never read an achiever -- backchaining reads achievers from lp, written once at first insertion; reached_facts achievers are dead). Moved the cheapest-path check inline into project/join/product (removed the batch newfacts buffer), move-insert kept facts into reached_facts. grounder_atoms/pushes FLAT vs baseline (identical productions => behavior-preserving; 62/62 checks pass). Win is 'cheaper' not 'fewer'. Contemporaneous A/B vs fresh HEAD (52.976 median): KEEP, confidence 9.09x, 5.87%. peak_mem 68->64MB.

Vendor utils/small_vector.h (inline-buffer vector for trivially-copyable T) and back datalog::Arguments with small_vector<Term,4> instead of std::vector<Term>. Typical atom arities (genome-edit-distance, the dominant ~59% domain, has arity <=2) now keep their argument terms inline, removing the per-produced-fact heap allocation (~47.6M Arguments copies in join/project/product). small_vector operator== is element-wise via Term::operator== and iterators are raw Term*, so Fact hashing/equality is byte-for-byte identical -> grounder_atoms/pushes FLAT (deterministic counters unchanged; behavior preserved; 62/62 checks pass). Fixed join.h JoiningVariables to qualify std::find/std::distance (raw-pointer iterators kill the prior std ADL). Win is 'cheaper'. Contemporaneous A/B vs fresh HEAD cf27d27 (49.697 median): KEEP, confidence 126x, 20.2%. Note: an earlier 65s single-sweep reading was contaminated by leftover background perf+search processes (env blocks perf sampling: 0 samples); killed them, load settled, re-measured clean.

Back datalog::Achievers with utils::small_vector<int,2> instead of std::vector<int>, and add direct int(a) and int(a,b) constructors so the hot project (1-atom body) and join (2-atom body) achiever bodies are built inline with NO heap allocation -- previously each newly reached fact (~22.4M) allocated a 1-2 element int vector. Product rules (body >2) keep a std::vector-range constructor and spill transparently. Achievers are not part of Fact hash/equality, so grounder_atoms/pushes stay FLAT (deterministic; behavior preserved; 62/62 checks pass; plan costs unchanged). Win is 'cheaper'. Contemporaneous A/B vs fresh HEAD fe9b548 (39.588 median): KEEP, confidence 81.5x, 16.3%. peak_mem 64->55MB.

Change JoinHashKey from std::vector<int> to utils::small_vector<int,3>; the key is rebuilt once per (popped fact, matching join rule) and joins share few vars (genome-edit-distance joins on <=2), so it now stays inline (no heap) both as the per-firing probe key and as the stored map key. Added a local JoinHashKeyHash (utils::hash_range, identical hash values to the prior VectorHash<int>) since utils::VectorHash is hard-typed to std::vector; added small_vector::reserve. hash/eq identical -> grounder_atoms/pushes FLAT (behavior preserved; 62/62 checks pass). Win is 'cheaper'. Contemporaneous A/B vs fresh HEAD 924e16e (32.878 median): KEEP, confidence 31.5x, 10.1%.

Idea #3: replace the cheaper-path erase+insert in process_new_fact with an in-place cost mutation (const_cast<Fact&>(*it).set_cost; cost is not part of the Fact key, so the set stays valid). Counters FLAT (behavior preserved). But DISCARD: contemporaneous A/B vs HEAD f8ede57 (29.369 median) gave candidate 29.680 median = -1.1% (slightly worse, within noise, confidence 2.9x but wrong direction). The strictly-cheaper-path branch is too infrequent (most of the 22.4M pushes are first-reaches, not cost improvements) for removing its erase+insert to register. Reverted.

Rewrite utils::small_vector with a union-based SBO layout (inline buffer unioned with heap pointer; capacity_ discriminates inline vs heap), removing the redundant 8-byte data_ pointer. Shrinks every Fact ~16 bytes (Arguments 32->24, Achievers 24->16). Counters FLAT (rewrite correct; behavior preserved). DETERMINISTIC memory win: peak_mem 55->48MB (-13%). But DISCARD on the PRIMARY metric: pooled 10+10 A/B vs HEAD f8ede57 gives only 0.32% (below 1.11% noise floor), confidence 1.15x and FALLING as reps grow -> no confirmable time win. INSIGHT: reached_facts is local to each ground() call and cleared per state eval, so each set is small/cache-resident regardless of Fact size; the runs 3-5 wins came from removing ALLOCATIONS (malloc/free cost), not object size. => size/N-tuning is a dead end here; future wins must remove allocations or cut operation count (the 'fewer'/productions lever). Reverted (kept the simpler pointer-based small_vector).

Reserve the grounder's local reached_facts (phmap::flat_hash_set<Fact>) up front to the previous ground() call's final size, instead of growing it from empty every call. The grounder is re-run once per state on similar reachable sets, so this skips the ~log(n) doubling rehashes (each rehashing all current elements) that the set otherwise pays per call -- targets allocation/rehash COUNT (the confirmed lever from run 7's insight), not object size. Added member reached_size_hint, set from reached_facts.size() at both return points. reached_facts is only probed by key / never iterated for productions, so capacity is behaviour-neutral -> grounder_atoms/pushes FLAT (62/62 checks pass). Win is 'cheaper'. Contemporaneous A/B vs fresh HEAD f8ede57 (29.822 median): KEEP, confidence 14.1x, 5.8%.

Stop destroying+rebuilding each JoinRule's hash tables every ground() call. JoinRule::clean_up was 'hash_table_indices = JoinHashTable()' (frees both phmap maps); now it calls reset_for_next_call() which records the previous call's key counts, clears the two outer maps in place, and reserves them to those counts. Same proven reserve pattern as run 8 (reached_facts) applied to the join hash outer maps -> skips the doubling rehashes the maps pay refilling from empty per call. Tables end up empty+pre-sized, functionally identical to fresh -> grounder_atoms/pushes FLAT (62/62 checks pass). (Inner per-key sets are still rebuilt; only the outer maps are reused.) Win is 'cheaper'. Contemporaneous A/B vs fresh HEAD 3377ad4 (27.910 median): KEEP, confidence 9.0x, 4.5%.

Attempted a 'fewer productions' win: when a join rule's inverse (partner) condition contributes NO variable to the head, every partner yields the same head atom, so emit a single head (first partner's achiever + min partner cost = byte-identical lp state) instead of one-per-partner. Detected statically per join firing. RESULT: grounder_atoms/pushes EXACTLY UNCHANGED (47644479/22398404) -> the collapse branch NEVER fired anywhere in the suite, and no trajectory moved. Reason: normal-form transformation (full_reducer/yannakakis semijoin reduction) already projects away non-propagating variables, so every join condition contributes to its head; the non-contributing case does not exist here. The added per-firing detection loop is pure overhead (single quiet sweep 28.1s vs ~26.7s). DISCARD by deterministic proof of zero benefit (no A/B needed). Lesson: the same-head-join 'fewer' lever is empty on this suite; semijoin reduction already did it.

CUMULATIVE CHECKPOINT after runs 2-5,8,9 (6 keeps). Contemporaneous A/B of current HEAD 221da57 vs the ORIGINAL baseline f2ff317 in one quiet window: baseline median 52.930s -> current 26.641s = 49.7% faster (~2x speedup), confidence 319x. grounder_atoms/pushes IDENTICAL to baseline (47644479/22398404) => every kept change is behaviour-preserving (same productions, same plans). peak_mem 68->55MB. All wins came from removing per-fact heap allocations (Arguments/Achievers/JoinHashKey inline small_vector) and skipping per-call hash-container rehashing (reserve reached_facts + reuse/pre-size join tables). Not a metric change; logged for the record.

Fuse the reached_facts find+insert into a single emplace probe in process_new_fact (halves the ~22.4M new-branch double-probes), with const_cast in-place cost mutation on the cheaper branch. Byte-identical: grounder_atoms/pushes FLAT, zero trajectory moves. But DISCARD: A/B vs HEAD 221da57 (26.505 median) gives candidate 26.588 = -0.3% (neutral/slightly worse). The saved probes are offset by the ~25.2M extra inline-Fact moves emplace does constructing+discarding a temp on every duplicate (53% of productions); the phmap SwissTable probe (compact control-byte scan) is too cheap for halving probe count to win this trade. Reverted. INSIGHT: operation-count reduction on the reached_facts probe is also tapped out -- the probe is already SwissTable-efficient.

Reserve backchain_from_goal's local achieved_atoms set (phmap::flat_hash_set<int>) to the previous backchain's size (new Datalog member backchain_reserve_hint), same pattern as run 8. Byte-identical (counters FLAT). But DISCARD: pooled 10+10 A/B vs HEAD 221da57 gives 0.55% (below 1.91% noise floor), confidence 1.32x and falling. Backchain only visits the relaxed-plan subset (much smaller than the 47.6M grounding productions), so reserving its set is within noise. Its main per-atom allocation (GroundAtom = std::vector<int> in add_useful_atom) can't be inlined -- GroundAtom is a core type used in 107 sites across states/novelty/successor-gen, far out of grounder scope. Reverted. This exhausts the reserve targets: reached_facts (run8 KEEP) + join tables (run9 KEEP) were the hot ones; backchain/initial_facts/EDB are too light.

JoinHashTable::get_entries looked up partners with operator[] (hash_table[key]), which INSERTS an empty JoinHashEntry whenever the key has no partner yet -- which is the common case while a join is still filling and permanent for one-sided keys. That polluted both join maps with empty entries (extra inserts + rehashing + larger maps + larger run-9 reserve). Switched to find() returning a static empty JoinHashEntry on miss: byte-identical (a missing key == an empty entry == no partners), grounder_atoms/pushes FLAT, 62/62 checks pass. peak_mem 55->51MB confirms the pollution was real. Win is 'cheaper'. Contemporaneous A/B vs fresh HEAD 221da57 (26.718 median): KEEP, confidence 4.35x, 1.5%.

MapVariablePosition (body-var -> head-position map, consulted once per body argument per produced head in project/join/product) used std::unordered_map<Term,int> (node-based, pointer-chasing) AND get_head_position_of_arg did TWO lookups (has_variable=count then at). Switched the map to phmap::flat_hash_map (SwissTable, cache-friendly) and added position_of() doing a SINGLE find. Tens of millions of calls in the hot loops. Byte-identical (same mapping) -> grounder_atoms/pushes FLAT, 62/62 checks pass. Win is 'cheaper'. Contemporaneous A/B vs fresh HEAD 359d50e (26.396 median): KEEP, confidence 31x, 10.3%. NB: a std::unordered_map on the hot path was a hidden bottleneck the allocation work had masked.

Precompute per (rule,condition) the head position of each argument (object/non-head folded to -1), lazily cached in RuleBase::condition_head_positions. The join position-fill and partner-fill inner loops now index this array instead of calling get_head_position_of_arg (a variable_position hash lookup) per argument per produced head -- tens of millions of lookups removed, replaced by branch+indexed write. Byte-identical (head_pos[i]!=-1 == old pos!=-1 && !is_object) -> grounder_atoms/pushes FLAT, 62/62 checks pass. Win is 'cheaper'. Contemporaneous A/B vs fresh HEAD 4a42d75 (23.786 median): KEEP, confidence 29x, 5.1%. (project keeps get_head_position_of_arg: it also does a constant-match check on object args.)

VariableSource had a const get_table() that returned the table BY VALUE (a full std::vector<pair<int,int>> copy). backchain's get_instantiation_of_variable does variable_table.get_table()[idx] per variable per useful atom (recursively), copying the whole table just to read one entry, and extract_variable_instantiation does get_table().size(). Changed the const overload to return const&. Byte-identical results -> grounder_atoms/pushes FLAT, 62/62 checks pass. Small win (backchain is light) but confirmable: tight A/B vs HEAD 592b0bc (22.631 median) -> KEEP, confidence 2.52x, 0.4%. Strictly-better code (no copy).

Reuse run-15's precomputed condition head positions in the project rule's loop too: non-object args index head_positions[i] instead of calling get_head_position_of_arg; object args keep their inline constant-match check. Byte-identical (counters FLAT, 62/62 checks pass). Small (project is less hot than join in this suite) but confirmable: A/B vs HEAD e7b30a7 (22.649 median) -> KEEP, confidence 3.47x, 0.6%.

Remove a redundant temporary in DatalogAtom's ctor: arguments(Arguments(std::move(arguments))) -> arguments(std::move(arguments)) (two inline-Arguments moves -> one), on every Fact construction (~47.6M). Byte-identical (counters FLAT). DISCARD: A/B (noisy window, load just spiked to 2.29) gives 0.23%, confidence 0.60x = within noise. The inline small_vector<Term,4> move is a ~32-byte memcpy; removing one per Fact is ~0.7% theoretical max and didn't clear noise. Strictly-better code but not a measurable metric win, so reverted per the time-gate. (A future cleanup could take it for code quality.)

CUMULATIVE CHECKPOINT after 11 keeps (runs 2-5,8,9,13-17). Contemporaneous A/B of current HEAD ac968eb vs ORIGINAL baseline f2ff317: baseline median 53.301s -> current 22.705s = 57.4% faster (2.35x speedup). grounder_atoms/pushes IDENTICAL to baseline (47644479/22398404) => every kept change behaviour-preserving. peak_mem 68->51MB. Two waves: (1) remove per-fact heap allocs (inline small_vectors) + skip per-call hash rehashing (reserve/reuse); (2) after that unmasked them, fix slow data structures (std::unordered_map->flat_hash_map for head positions = 10%), accidental work (operator[] empty-entry pollution), redundant per-element lookups (precompute head positions), and a by-value table return. Logged for the record.
